summary: High‑energy set with scorching Scarlet>Touch>Mr Fantasy and strong second‑set jams
blurb: The show erupts with a fiery Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Dear Mr. Fantasy sequence and a powerful second‑set featuring The Wheel, Space and The Other One. Jerry’s guitar shines while the rhythm section drives the high‑energy flow. Recording quality varies, but the performance remains solid.
review: On June 29, 1984 the Dead opened Blossom Music Center with a first set that crackled from the opening “Bertha” into a tight run of rockers – “Greatest Story Ever Told,” “They Love Each Other,” and a soaring “Bird Song.” The highlight was the second‑set monster: a scorching “Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Dear Mr. Fantasy” segue that had Garcia’s lead vocal on “Mr. Fantasy” fiery (if occasionally lyric‑slipped) and his guitar slicing through the mix. From there the band rode a wave of intensity through “Man Smart, Woman Smarter,” a crisp drum‑and‑space interlude, then into “The Wheel,” “The Other One,” and a rollicking “Johnny B. Goode” that kept the crowd dancing late into the night. While audience recordings struggle to capture Phil’s low‑end bass and an occasional off‑key vocal on “Dear Mr. Fantasy,” the rhythm section – Mickey, Billy and Brent – stays locked in, with drum separation surprisingly clear even in the lawn tape. Overall the performance is a solid high‑energy snapshot of the mid‑80s Dead, especially notable as their first show at the brand‑new venue.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are marked mixed and one positive; the positives focus on high‑energy jams, strong Scarlet>Touch>Mr Fantasy sequence and solid drum work, while negatives note buried bass and an off‑key vocal on Dear Mr. Fantasy. The overall tone leans modestly positive, warranting a moderate +0.6 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape captures the party vibe and surprisingly clear drum separation, making it the most representative source despite thin low end.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Dear Mr. Fantasy sequence.
High‑energy second set with The Wheel, Space, and The Other One.
Tight transitions and strong drum work drive the show’s momentum.
First appearance at a brand‑new venue adds extra excitement.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers fiery guitar throughout, with a standout vocal on Mr. Fantasy despite occasional lyric slips; his playing remains strong even when the vocal on Dear Mr. Fantasy falters.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass is largely buried in audience mixes, making his thunder hard to hear, though no specific performance issues are noted beyond that.
band_performance.Mickey: The drum work is remarkably clear for an audience source; Mickey helps drive the high‑energy flow with crisp percussion.
band_performance.Billy: Billy’s drumming locks in tightly with Mickey, providing a solid rhythmic foundation especially evident during the second‑set jams.
